title: README hero uses animated demo GIF
summary: The README hero should use the animated docs/assets/kage demo.gif, not the static kage viewer.svg. The GIF demonstrates the actual Kage install/recall/review workflow and matches the launch promise better than a static t
body: The README hero should use the animated docs/assets/kage-demo.gif, not the static kage-viewer.svg. The GIF demonstrates the actual Kage install/recall/review workflow and matches the launch promise better than a static terminal poster.

context.fact: The README hero should use docs/assets/kage-demo.gif rather than the static kage-viewer.svg poster.
context.why: The animated GIF demonstrates the real install, recall, and review workflow better than a static terminal image.
context.trigger: Recall when editing the README hero, launch media, or viewer/demo assets.
context.action: Keep the animated demo GIF as the primary README hero unless a newer real workflow demo replaces it.
context.verification: Repo-local capture grounded to README.md, docs/assets/kage-demo.gif, and docs/assets/kage-viewer.svg.
context.risk_if_forgotten: Agents may revert to a less convincing static hero and weaken the launch promise.
context.stale_when: A newer canonical animated demo asset replaces docs/assets/kage-demo.gif.
